# Timeline

## Start-up Phase

* **Reporting Framework**: Establish a bi-weekly internal review process to track milestone progress.

## Technical Delivery

The proposed work will be delivered over a 10-week period and involve approximately 360 hours of expert developer time. The work is structured into three phases:

* **Phase 1: Memory and State Management (Weeks 1-4)**:
  * Development of automated state persistence and restart checkpointing.
  * Development of the serialization engine.
  * Development of dynamic sub-graph injection for self-correcting flows.
  * Development of the validation and pruning layers for network preparedness.

* **Phase 2: Environment Management (Weeks 5-8)**:
  * Development of Docker Integration
  * Development of Git worktree for state tracking in containers
  * Development of compatibility mapping for Singularity and Apptainer

* **Phase 3: Validation & Community Management (Weeks 9-10)**:
  * Finalization of Agents and Skills Management tools into lifecycle modules.
  * Documentation, bug fixing, and release processing.

```{mermaid}
gantt
    title HydraR 10-Week Development Timeline
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
    axisFormat  %m/%y
    
    section Memory and State Management
    Restart Checkpointing                   :active, dev1, 2026-05-01, 6d
    Intelligent Hashing and State Retrieval :dev2, after dev1, 6d
    Dynamic Network Repair & Validation     :dev3, after dev2, 6d

    section Environment Management
    Docker Integration                      :dev4, after dev3, 6d
    Git worktree in Docker                  :dev5, after dev4, 6d
    Singularity & Apptainer                 :dev6, after dev5, 6d
    
    section Validation & Community Management
    Agents & Skills Management              :dev7, after dev6, 6d
    Documentation & Release                 :dev8, after dev7, 6d
```

## Other Aspects

* **Hosting**: Code will be hosted on GitHub under the `APAF-bioinformatics` organization.
* **Conference**: We will attend and present HydraR at relevant conferences to engage with the bioinformatics community.
* **Social media**: We will utilize social media to engage with the bioinformatics community. 
* **Publicity**: We will share quarterly updates on the R Consortium blog.
* **Community Engagement**: We will share undergo the rOpenSci peer-review process and transition to a community-maintained model.

## Budget & Funding Plan

Total requested budget: **$14,400 USD**. We request funding for expert software developers to work on this project over a 10-week period (approximately 360 hours).

| Milestone                             | Description              | Estimated Hours | Funding     |
| :---                                  | :---                     | :---:           | ---:        |
| **Memory and State Management**       | [Details of Milestone 1] | 140             | $5,760      |
| **Environment Management**            | [Details of Milestone 2] | 140             | $5,760      |
| **Validation & Community Management** | [Details of Milestone 3] | 80              | $2,880      |
| **Total**                             |                          | **360**         | **$14,400** |

|                                   | **R Consortium**<br>**Cash (USD)** | **Macquarie University**<br>**In-kind** |
| :---                              | ---:                               | ---:                                    |
| Aidan Tay (1.0 FTE)               | $14,400                            | -                                       |
| Chi Nam (Ignatius) Pang (0.2 FTE) | -                                  | $3,431                                  |
| Total                             | **$14,400**                        | **$3,431**                              |
